payload.text:
Build a clickable mobile-first prototype for an educational support platform called SchoolBridge designed to help schools detect struggling students early and connect them with academic and emotional support. The prototype should include three user roles: Student, Teacher, and Parent, each with a separate dashboard and navigation flow.
Design style: clean, friendly, modern, flat UI, rounded cards and buttons, soft blue/green/orange color palette, accessible typography, minimal clutter, supportive and non-threatening tone.
Student Flow & Screens
Create a Student Dashboard screen with:
Welcome header with student name
Subject progress cards with horizontal progress bars (Math, English, Science)
Risk flag indicators (green/yellow/red)
“Attention Needed” alert box listing missing tasks and low quiz scores
Large primary button section labeled “Need Help?” with 4 buttons:
I don’t understand this lesson
I’m falling behind
I need to talk to a teacher
I feel stressed
Upcoming tasks list
Bottom navigation: Home / Progress / Help / Messages
Create a Help Request screen:
Let student select subject
Text box to describe problem
Toggle for anonymous request
Submit button
Create a Progress Detail screen:
Charts for weekly performance
Assignment completion rate
Teacher feedback notes
Teacher Flow & Screens
Create a Teacher Dashboard with:
Class overview metrics cards (average score, missing work %, engagement level)
Student Alert List sorted by risk level
Each student row shows: name, risk color, issue summary
Quick action buttons:
Message student
Assign support resources
Recommend peer tutor
Create Student Detail screen:
Performance chart
Missing work list
Help requests history
Teacher note entry box
Parent Flow & Screens
Create a Parent Dashboard:
Child progress summary cards by subject
Simple color status indicators
Teacher notes panel
“How to help at home” tips section
Contact teacher button
System Features to Simulate
Notification badges on alerts and messages
Color-coded risk system
Example dummy student data
Clickable navigation between screens
Sample charts and progress visuals
Friendly microcopy (supportive wording, not punitive)
Accessibility Requirements
Large tap targets
High contrast text
Simple language labels
Icon + text for all major actions
Build it as an interactive prototype with realistic sample data and working navigation between all screens.

**Defining Mobile-First Platform**

I'm now zeroing in on defining the "SchoolBridge" prototype's core. My focus is on the mobile-first approach, the three distinct user roles (Student, Teacher, Parent) and their respective dashboards. I am thinking about essential user flows, and a clean, modern, friendly UI aesthetic (Tailwind CSS will be the foundation).


**Outlining Key Features**

I'm solidifying the feature set now, thinking in terms of specific screen elements. I'm focusing on defining the key areas, like Student's dashboard for progress, flags, and help triggers; Teacher's dashboard for class metrics and alerts; and Parent's dashboard for child summary and contact. I'm also planning the application structure, including the components, screens, and global styles using Tailwind CSS.


**Refining App Components**

I'm now diving into the component details. I've broken down the dashboards and workflows. On the student side, "Need Help?" and "Attention Needed" are key, while teachers focus on the "Student Alert List" and "Class Metrics". Parents need "How to help at home" and child status. I'm visualizing subject cards, help buttons, and the help request form. Weekly charts, dashboards, and detailed student performance views are also in progress. I'm settling on the Tailwind styling, looking at soft, rounded corners, soft shadows, padding, font sizes, and consistent navigation icons and labels. I am also working out the risk color logic.

entries.description: Main application component with role and screen management
